task_completion.description: Verify that the Sygic GPS Navigation app is launched.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Sygic GPS Navigation app interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Speed Limit display setting is enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Settings > Notifications > Speed Limits menu with the display toggle switch in the ON position.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Speedometer setting is enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Settings > Cockpit menu with the Speedometer toggle switch in the ON position.

task_completion.description: Verify that the user has navigated back to the main map view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the primary map navigation screen of the Sygic app.

task_completion.description: Verify that the speedometer widget is visible on the main map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A speedometer widget displaying a number (likely '0' at standstill) along with 'km/h' or 'mph' is clearly visible on the map screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the speed limit indicator is visible on the main map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A speed limit sign icon is clearly visible on the map screen.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the graphical user interface to navigate settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show step-by-step GUI navigation through the app's menus rather than jumping directly to the end state via terminal commands.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual Sygic GPS Navigation application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches the genuine Sygic app (com.sygic.aura) and not a web browser, image editor, or mock interface.

integrity.description: Ensure the speedometer and speed limit overlays are rendered by the app.
integrity.visual_evidence: The overlays appear as native UI elements integrated into the map view, without signs of being artificially pasted or edited onto the screenshot.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task targets Sygic GPS Navigation. The Speed limit display toggle is located at Settings > Notifications > Speed Limits. The Speedometer controls are located in Settings > Cockpit. On the main map, the Speedometer displays the current speed (e.g., '0' at standstill) and units ('km/h' or 'mph'). The Speed limit feature displays a speed limit sign on the screen.
